name: Hamburg (Germany)

colors.symbolism: the colour of the castle, also Hanseatic

description: A red field bearing a white three-towered castle with a closed gate; the central tower carries a cross and the side towers are topped with stars.
history: Hamburg's civil flag was officially adopted on 14 May 1751 (and re-confirmed in 1952), making it one of the oldest municipal flags in Germany. The castle motif derives from Hamburg's medieval seal and represents the city's status as a free Hanseatic port; the state flag (Staatsflagge) and admiralty flag add a more elaborate version of the arms.

features.symbolism: The castle is documented as Hamburg's symbol since at least the 13th century; the central tower bears a cross, the side towers are topped with six-pointed stars representing the Virgin Mary, patroness of Hamburg cathedral.
